title: [StandardToHandshake] Taking fork/sink materialization out of conversion pass
body:
Currently, the `StandardToHandshake` conversion pass performs fork/sink materialization during the conversion process. [As a comment in the code says](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/main/include/circt/Conversion/StandardToHandshake.h#L178), it would be better for this do be done at user's request after initial dataflow conversion.

I'm trying to take a shot at this. More broadly, I'm trying to address the open issues on `StandardToHandshake` and figured doing that first might simplify other fixes down the line. The main issue with removing fork/sink materialization is that [connectToMemory](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/main/lib/Conversion/StandardToHandshake/StandardToHandshake.cpp#L1626) depends on it, which is what I'm trying to address currently. 

Are there other parts of the implementation that would be affected by the removal of fork/sink materialization during dialect conversion? I don't see any right now but if there is one and you can point me to it it would be super helpful!
